welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.body: Welcome to TGATB Habit Tracker ("The Good and The Bad", "we", "us", "our"). By using our web and mobile applications, you agree to these Terms of Service.

use.title: Use of the App
use.item1: You must be at least 13 years old to use the app.
use.item2: You agree not to misuse the app or attempt to access it in unauthorized ways.

userContent.title: User Content
userContent.item1: You are responsible for the content you add (habits, notes, etc.).
userContent.item2: We reserve the right to remove content that violates these terms or applicable laws.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.item1: All app content, trademarks, and code are owned by us or our licensors.
intellectualProperty.item2: You may not copy, modify, or distribute our content without permission.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.body: The app uses third-party services (Google Cloud, Firebase, etc.). Your use of these services is subject to their respective terms.

limitation.title: Limitation of Liability
limitation.body: The app is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of the app.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: We may suspend or terminate your access to the app for violation of these terms.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms
changes.body: We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. Changes will be posted in the app and on our website.

meta.title: Privacy Policy - TGATB Habit Tracker
meta.description: Privacy Policy for The Good and The Bad: Habit Tracker

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: TGATB Habit Tracker ("The Good and The Bad") is a minimalist habit tracking application developed by adsurkasur. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle your information when you use our web application at www.tgatb.click and our Android mobile application.

dataYouProvide.title: Data You Provide
dataYouProvide.habitData: Habit data: Names, types (good/bad), completion logs, and streaks you create within the app. This data is stored locally on your device by default.
dataYouProvide.settings: Settings preferences: Dark mode, language, motivator personality, and other app preferences stored locally on your device.
dataYouProvide.googleAccount: Google account information (optional): If you choose to sign in with Google for cloud backup, we access your name, email address, and profile picture solely for authentication and Google Drive backup functionality.

dataCollectedAutomatically.title: Data Collected Automatically
dataCollectedAutomatically.analytics: Analytics data (with consent): We use Vercel Analytics and Firebase Analytics to collect anonymous usage statistics such as page views, device type, and general interaction patterns. Analytics are only initialized after you provide consent.
dataCollectedAutomatically.errorTracking: Error tracking (optional): If enabled, Sentry collects crash reports and error logs to help us improve app stability. This is only active when analytics consent is given and a Sentry DSN is configured.

dataNotCollected.title: Data We Do NOT Collect
dataNotCollected.noPayments: We do not collect payment information
dataNotCollected.noLocation: We do not collect location data
dataNotCollected.noContacts: We do not collect contacts or phone data
dataNotCollected.noAds: We do not serve advertisements or collect ad-related data
dataNotCollected.noSelling: We do not sell your data to third parties

dataStorage.title: How Your Data Is Stored
dataStorage.local: Local storage (default): All habit data and settings are stored locally on your device using browser localStorage (web) or Capacitor Preferences (Android). No data leaves your device unless you explicitly enable cloud backup.
dataStorage.cloudBackup: Cloud backup (optional): If you sign in with Google, you may choose to back up your data to your own Google Drive. Your data is stored as a file in your personal Google Drive – we do not have access to your Google Drive files.

thirdPartyServices.title: Third-Party Services
thirdPartyServices.intro: We use the following third-party services:
thirdPartyServices.firebase: Firebase Authentication: For Google sign-in. Governed by Google's Privacy Policy.
thirdPartyServices.firebaseAnalytics: Firebase Analytics: For anonymous usage statistics (consent required).
thirdPartyServices.vercelAnalytics: Vercel Analytics: For anonymous web analytics (consent required).
thirdPartyServices.googleDrive: Google Drive API: For optional cloud backup, accessing only your own app-created files.
thirdPartyServices.sentry: Sentry (optional): For error and crash reporting when enabled.

rightsControls.title: Your Rights and Controls
rightsControls.analyticsConsent: Analytics consent: You can enable or disable analytics at any time in the app settings.
rightsControls.dataDeletion: Data deletion: You can delete all your habit data from the app settings ("Delete All Habits"). Since data is stored locally, clearing your browser data or uninstalling the app will also remove all data.
rightsControls.cloudBackupControl: Cloud backup control: You can sign out at any time to stop cloud backup. Your Google Drive backup files can be managed directly from your Google Drive.
rightsControls.dataExport: Data export: You can export your data as a JSON file from the app settings at any time.

deleteData.title: Delete Your Data
deleteData.intro: TGATB Habit Tracker stores your habit data locally on your device. You can delete all your data at any time without needing to delete your account:
deleteData.steps:
1. Open the app
2. Tap the menu icon (hamburger menu) in the top-left corner
3. Scroll down to Habit Management
4. Tap "Delete All Habits"
5. Confirm the deletion when prompted
deleteData.willDeleteItems: All your habits and their names, All completion logs and streak data, All history records
deleteData.willNotDeleteItems: Your app settings (dark mode, language, etc.) — these reset when you clear app data or uninstall, Any backup files previously saved to your Google Drive — you can delete those directly from your Google Drive
deleteData.alternative: Alternatively, you can uninstall the app or clear app data from your device settings to remove all locally stored information.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Your Account
deleteAccount.intro: If you signed in with Google and want to completely remove your account and all associated data, follow these steps:
deleteAccount.appSteps:
1. Open the app
2. Tap the menu icon (hamburger menu) in the top-left corner
3. Scroll down to Habit Management and tap "Delete All Habits" to remove your data
4. Scroll to Account & Data and tap "Logout" to sign out
5. Uninstall the app to remove all remaining local data
deleteAccount.googleDriveSteps:
To remove your Google Drive backup files:
1. Go to Google Drive
2. Search for "tgatb" or "habit" to find backup files
3. Delete the backup files
deleteAccount.revokeSteps:
To revoke the app's access to your Google account:
1. Go to Google Account Permissions
2. Find "TGATB Habit Tracker" in the list
3. Click "Remove Access"
deleteAccount.completion: After completing these steps, all your data and account access will be fully removed. If you need assistance, contact us at adsurkasur.dev@gmail.com.

dataSecurity.title: Data Security
dataSecurity.body: We implement reasonable security measures to protect your data. Your habit data is stored locally on your device and is not transmitted to our servers. Cloud backup data is stored in your personal Google Drive using Google's security infrastructure. However, no method of electronic storage is 100% secure.

childrenPrivacy.title: Children's Privacy
childrenPrivacy.body: Our app is not directed at children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided us with personal information, please contact us.

policyChanges.title: Changes to This Policy
policyChanges.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.

contactUs.title: Contact Us
contactUs.body: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at: adsurkasur.dev@gmail.com

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.body: TGATB Habit Tracker is open source under the Apache 2.0 License. You can review the complete source code at: github.com/adsurkasur/tgatb-habit-tracker
